title: Conditional Statements
overview: Programs need to make decisions. Conditional statements let your code choose different paths based on conditions.

blocks.title: Making Decisions with if
blocks.body: The `if` statement lets you run code only when a condition is True. If False, the code is skipped.
bullets: Write if followed by a condition and a colon
The code to run goes on the next line, indented 4 spaces
Python uses indentation to show what code belongs to the if
If True, the indented block runs; if False, it is skipped

blocks.title: if is Like a Traffic Light
blocks.text: Green light (True) → you go. Red light (False) → you stop. The if statement checks the condition and decides whether to run the code or skip it.

blocks.title: Basic if Statement
blocks.code:
age = 18
if age >= 18:
    print("You are an adult")
    print("You can vote!")
blocks.expectedOutput:
You are an adult
You can vote!

blocks.title: Voting Eligibility
blocks.instructions: Ask for age. If 18+, print "You are eligible to vote!"
blocks.starterCode:
age = int(input("Enter your age: "))
if age >= 18:
blocks.hint: Use if age >= 18: followed by an indented print() statement.
blocks.expectedOutput:
Enter your age: 20
You are eligible to vote!

blocks.title: Two Paths with if-else
blocks.body: When you need to choose between two options, use if-else. One block runs if True, another if False.
bullets: Only ONE of the two blocks runs, never both

blocks.title: if-else in Action
Multiple Conditions with elif
blocks.body: When you have more than two possibilities, use elif. You can chain as many as needed.
bullets: elif checks the next (if previous was False)
else runs only if ALL conditions were False

blocks.title: elif Chain
Truthy and Falsy Values
blocks.body: Some values are treated as True (truthy) and others as False (falsy) in conditions.
bullets: Truthy: everything else — non-zero numbers, non-empty strings
Lets you write concise conditions like if name:

blocks.title: Truthy and Falsy
Grade Calculator
blocks.instructions: Ask for marks. Print grade: 90+ = A, 80-89 = B, 70-79 = C, 60-69 = D, below 60 = F.
blocks.hint: Check the highest grade first (>= 90), then work down.

keyTakeaways.text: if runs code only when a condition is True
if-else provides two paths: one for True, one for False
elif chains multiple conditions in sequence
Truthy values: non-zero numbers, non-empty strings
Falsy values: 0, None, empty strings, empty lists

whatYouLearned: How to write if statements with proper indentation
How to use if-else for two-way decisions
How to chain multiple conditions with elif
What truthy and falsy values are
How to build a complete grade calculator
